action.reasoning: Observation: ConvTasNet training crashes on ValueError (too many values to unpack) due to incorrect tensor shape in val loop: unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0) on already-batched (1,T) makes (1,1,1,T)=4D instead of (1,1,T). MetricGAN+ + loudness=-25 remains best (test PESQ=3.137, val=2.862 flat across strategies). test_enhance.py ran successfully, confirming setup. Analysis: Root cause is val data handling (DataLoader adds batch dim); fixing to .unsqueeze(1) + proper est.squeeze() will allow training. MetricGAN+ is task-specific/strong; beating it requires well-tuned ConvTasNet (more epochs, fixed loss, better chunks) but low-risk to restore best score now while enabling future training. Decision: Provide corrected full train.py (fixed shapes, best-model saving by val PESQ, metrics logging per epoch, 10 epochs for measurement), keep predict.py on MetricGAN+, run test+train (short), then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint to restore/confirm best PESQ before longer training runs.
